# UC-21.8.15 · Asterisk CDR Codec and Post-Dial Delay Anomalies

## Description

Post-dial delay (PDD) approximated as `duration - billsec` on answered calls highlights SIP routing or transcoding stalls. A rising median PDD or exploding mix of codecs per hour often precedes MOS complaints even when trunks show green.

## Value

Speeds root-cause analysis for voice quality tickets by separating RTP issues from pure capacity problems using existing CDR fields.

## Implementation

Ensure `asterisk:cdr` field extraction matches your `Master.csv` column order—common positions documented in Asterisk samples. If using custom CDR fields for negotiated codec, map to `dstcodec`. Tune PDD threshold: mobile interconnection may legitimately be higher. Investigate codec_mix>3 as transcoding or misconfigured peering.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Consistent CSV headers in `cdr.conf`.
• Exclude conference bridges if they skew PDD.

Step 1 — Validate PDD vs PCAP on a test call.

Step 2 — Join with SIP `INVITE` logs if available.

Step 3 — Document carrier-specific baselines.

Step 4 — Feed ITSI KPI if using premium.

## SPL

```spl
index=voip sourcetype=asterisk:cdr
| eval billsec=tonumber(billsec), duration=tonumber(duration)
| eval pdd=duration-billsec
| eval codec=coalesce(lastdata, dstcodec)
| where disposition="ANSWERED" AND billsec>0
| bin _time span=1h
| stats median(pdd) as med_pdd, count as answered_calls, dc(codec) as codec_mix by _time, host
| where med_pdd > 4 OR codec_mix > 3
```

## Visualization

Line chart (med_pdd), Pie chart (codec share), Table (trunk groups).

## References

- [Asterisk Documentation — CDR](https://docs.asterisk.org/Operation/Logging/Call-Detail-Records-CDR/)
